title: Custom strategies
text: strategy family base class pure virtual methods. new strategy inherits appropriate base implements . example, new projection pursuit strategy needs implement optimize() (find best projection): key pieces: to_json() serializes strategy name parameters. used model persistence. from_json() deserializes JSON validates unexpected keys present. PPFOREST2_REGISTER_STRATEGY registers factory JSON deserialization finds automatically. display_name() returns human-readable label summaries. Factory function (my_method()) convenience wrapper. pattern applies variable selection strategies (select()), cutpoint strategies (cutpoint()), strategy families. See C++ documentation complete interface definitions examples. writing .cpp file, add core/src/models/CMakeLists.txt.
code: // File: core/src/models/strategies/pp/MyMethod.hpp #pragma once #include "models/strategies/pp/ProjectionPursuit.hpp" #include "models/strategies/Strategy.hpp" #include "utils/JsonValidation.hpp"  namespace ppforest2::pp {  struct MyMethod : public ProjectionPursuit {   explicit MyMethod(float alpha) : alpha_(alpha) {}    std::string display_name() const override { return "My method"; }    Result optimize(     const types::FeatureMatrix&  x,     const stats::GroupPartition& group_spec) const override {     // Find the optimal projector for the data.     // Return Result{ projector_vector, index_value }.     ...   }    nlohmann::json to_json() const override {     return {{"name", "my_method"}, {"alpha", alpha_}};   }    static ProjectionPursuit::Ptr from_json(const nlohmann::json& j) {     JsonReader{j, "my_method"}.only_keys({"name", "alpha"});     return my_method(j.at("alpha").get<float>());   }    PPFOREST2_REGISTER_STRATEGY(ProjectionPursuit, "my_method")  private:   const float alpha_; };  inline ProjectionPursuit::Ptr my_method(float alpha) {   return std::make_shared<MyMethod>(alpha); }  }  // namespace ppforest2::pp

title: Custom strategies
text: Write R function creates strategy list. name field must match C++ registration name exactly. constructor : Validate parameters reach C++. Catching errors early clear messages better C++ exception. Set S3 class pp_strategy, vars_strategy, cutpoint_strategy, stop_strategy, binarize_strategy, grouping_strategy. checked resolve_strategies(). Include display_name readable output summary(). Use parameter names to_json() C++. R list converted JSON passed from_json() C++ side.
code: #' My custom projection pursuit strategy. #' #' @param alpha A tuning parameter. #' @return A \code{pp_strategy} object. #' @export pp_my_method <- function(alpha = 1.0) {   if (!is.numeric(alpha) || length(alpha) != 1)     stop("`alpha` must be a single number.")    structure(     list(name = "my_method", display_name = "My method", alpha = alpha),     class = "pp_strategy"   ) }

title: Custom strategies
text: Create core/src/models/strategies/<family>/MyStrategy.hpp (.cpp needed). Inherit appropriate base class (ProjectionPursuit, VariableSelection, Cutpoint, StopRule, Binarization, Grouping). Implement pure virtual methods. Implement to_json() "name" field. Implement display_name() human-readable summaries. Add static Ptr from_json() key validation. Add PPFOREST2_REGISTER_STRATEGY(Base, "name"). Add factory function strategy’s namespace. Add .cpp core/src/models/CMakeLists.txt. Write tests MyStrategy.test.cpp (JSON round-trip + functional). Write R constructor function validation, display_name, correct S3 class. Export document R function.

title: Introduction to ppforest2
text: Train projection-pursuit tree iris dataset: tree splits linear projections features. Use summary() see variable importance: Predict new observations:
code: library(ppforest2)  tree <- pptr(Species ~ ., data = iris, seed = 0) tree #>  #> Call: pptr(formula = Species ~ ., data = iris, seed = 0) #>  #> Projection-Pursuit Oblique Decision Tree: #> If ([ 0 -0.04 0.03 0.03 ] * x) < 0.01580044: #>   Predict: setosa  #> Else: #>  If ([ 0 0.03 -0.06 -0.15 ] * x) < -0.4503323: #>    Predict: virginica  #>  Else: #>    Predict: versicolor summary(tree) #>  #> Projection-Pursuit Oblique Decision Tree #>  #> pp method: PDA (lambda=0.5) #> vars method: All variables #> cutpoint method: Mean of means #> stop rule: Pure node #> binarize method: Largest gap #> grouping method: By label #> leaf method: Majority vote #>  #>  #> Data Summary: #>   observations: 150  #>   features:     4  #>   groups:       3  #>   group names:  setosa, versicolor, virginica  #>   formula:      Species ~ Sepal.Length + Sepal.Width + Petal.Length + Petal.Width -      1  #>  #> Confusion Matrix: #>  #>             Predicted #> Actual       setosa versicolor virginica #>   setosa         50          0         0 #>   versicolor      0         47         3 #>   virginica       0          3        47 #>  #> Training error: 4% #>  #> Variable Importance: #>  #>       Variable         σ  Projection #> 1  Petal.Width 0.7622377 0.067165621 #> 2 Petal.Length 1.7652982 0.064908713 #> 3  Sepal.Width 0.4358663 0.011343628 #> 4 Sepal.Length 0.8280661 0.001772132 #>  #> Note: Variable importance was calculated using scaled coefficients (|a_j| * σ_j). #> Variable contributions can only be theoretically interpreted as such #> if the model was trained on scaled data. Scaling also changes the #> projection-pursuit optimization, which may affect the resulting tree. preds <- predict(tree, iris) table(predicted = preds, actual = iris$Species) #>             actual #> predicted    setosa versicolor virginica #>   setosa         50          0         0 #>   versicolor      0         47         3 #>   virginica       0          3        47

title: Introduction to ppforest2
text: Train forest 100 trees, considering 2 randomly chosen variables split: summary shows OOB (--bag) error estimate three variable importance measures: Projection: average absolute projection coefficient across splits Weighted: projection importance weighted number observations routed node Permuted: decrease OOB accuracy variable permuted Predict class probabilities:
code: forest <- pprf(Species ~ ., data = iris, size = 100, n_vars = 2, seed = 0) summary(forest) #>  #> Random Forest of Projection-Pursuit Oblique Decision Trees #>  #> Size: 100 trees #> pp method: PDA (lambda=0.5) #> vars method: Uniform random (count=2) #> cutpoint method: Mean of means #> stop rule: Pure node #> binarize method: Largest gap #> grouping method: By label #> leaf method: Majority vote #>  #>  #> Data Summary: #>   observations: 150  #>   features:     4  #>   groups:       3  #>   group names:  setosa, versicolor, virginica  #>   formula:      Species ~ Sepal.Length + Sepal.Width + Petal.Length + Petal.Width -      1  #>  #> Training Confusion Matrix: #>  #>             Predicted #> Actual       setosa versicolor virginica #>   setosa         50          0         0 #>   versicolor      0         48         2 #>   virginica       0          5        45 #>  #> Training error: 4.67% #>  #> OOB Confusion Matrix: #>  #>             Predicted #> Actual       setosa versicolor virginica #>   setosa         50          0         0 #>   versicolor      0         48         2 #>   virginica       0          4        46 #>  #> OOB error: 4% #>  #> Variable Importance: #>  #>       Variable         σ  Projection    Weighted   Permuted #> 1 Petal.Length 1.7652982 0.060925536 0.044617556 0.31747234 #> 2  Petal.Width 0.7622377 0.045294985 0.035571091 0.24510610 #> 3 Sepal.Length 0.8280661 0.013997004 0.006649137 0.06048262 #> 4  Sepal.Width 0.4358663 0.007664503 0.005792385 0.03662244 #>  #> Note: Variable importance was calculated using scaled coefficients (|a_j| * σ_j). #> Variable contributions can only be theoretically interpreted as such #> if the model was trained on scaled data. Scaling also changes the #> projection-pursuit optimization, which may affect the resulting tree. probs <- predict(forest, iris[1:5, ], type = "prob") probs #>   setosa versicolor virginica #> 1      1          0         0 #> 2      1          0         0 #> 3      1          0         0 #> 4      1          0         0 #> 5      1          0         0

title: Introduction to ppforest2
text: control, can pass strategy objects directly instead using shortcut parameters (lambda, n_vars, p_vars). equivalent makes strategy choice explicit: Available strategy constructors: pp_pda(lambda) — PDA projection pursuit (lambda = 0 LDA) vars_uniform(n_vars) vars_uniform(p_vars) — random variable selection vars_all() — use variables (default single trees) cutpoint_mean_of_means() — midpoint split rule (default) Strategy objects can also passed engine arguments parsnip:
code: # These two calls produce identical results: forest_shortcut <- pprf(Species ~ ., data = iris, size = 10, lambda = 0.5, n_vars = 2, seed = 0)  forest_explicit <- pprf(Species ~ ., data = iris, size = 10, pp = pp_pda(0.5), vars = vars_uniform(n_vars = 2), seed = 0)  all.equal(predict(forest_shortcut, iris), predict(forest_explicit, iris)) #> [1] TRUE library(parsnip)  spec <- pp_rand_forest(trees = 10) |>   set_engine("ppforest2", pp = pp_pda(0.5), vars = vars_uniform(n_vars = 2)) |>   set_mode("classification")  fit <- spec |> fit(Species ~ ., data = iris) predict(fit, iris[1:5, ]) #> # A tibble: 5 × 1 #>   .pred_class #>   <fct>       #> 1 setosa      #> 2 setosa      #> 3 setosa      #> 4 setosa      #> 5 setosa

what: Projection Pursuit Oblique Decision Trees and Random Forests
title: Projection Pursuit Oblique Decision Trees and Random Forests
text: ppforest2 provides projection pursuit oblique decision trees random forests classification. Instead splitting single variables, node projects data onto linear combination features, capturing structure axis-aligned trees miss. package wraps high-performance C++ core intended modern successor PPforest. Key capabilities: oblique splits via projection pursuit, multi-threaded forest training (OpenMP), cross-platform reproducibility, three variable importance measures (projection-based, weighted, permutation), LDA/PDA optimisation, OOB error estimation, parsnip / tidymodels integration.

what: Permuted variable importance for a random forest. — permuted_importance
title: Permuted variable importance for a random forest. — permuted_importance
text: feature, measures drop OOB accuracy (classification) increase normalised MSE (regression) randomly permuting feature across OOB observations. Computed lazily training data stored model; result cached.

title: Permuted variable importance for a random forest. — permuted_importance
Permuted variable importance for a random forest. — permuted_importance
text: model pprf forest model.

title: Permuted variable importance for a random forest. — permuted_importance
text: numeric vector, one entry per feature. Negative values   meaningful (see Sign semantics ).

title: Permuted variable importance for a random forest. — permuted_importance
text: **Sign semantics.** Entries may **negative**. error sentinel: means permuting feature degrade OOB fit average — feature's signal sits noise floor permutation procedure. Interpret negative near-zero entries "evidence importance"; rely ranking rather clipping zero normalizing. scale already comparable within fitted model.

title: Plot a pprf model. — plot.pprf
text: x pprf model. type Character string specifying plot type. "importance" (default) shows variable importance, "structure" shows specific tree embedded histograms, "projection" shows projected data node, "boundaries" shows decision boundaries specific tree. metric Character string selecting single importance metric plot: "projections", "weighted", "permuted".  NULL (default) shows available metrics side side separate panels.  used type = "importance". tree_index Integer index tree plot (1-based). used type = "structure", type = "projection", type = "boundaries". Defaults 1. node Integer index node projection plots. Defaults 1 (root). used type = "projection". ... Additional arguments passed internal plotting function.

what: Trains a Random Forest of Projection-Pursuit oblique decision trees. — pprf
title: Trains a Random Forest of Projection-Pursuit oblique decision trees. — pprf
text: function trains Random Forest Projection-Pursuit oblique decision tree using either formula data frame interface matrix-based interface. using formula interface, specify model formula data frame containing variables. matrix-based interface, provide matrices features labels directly. number trees controlled size parameter. tree trained stratified bootstrap sample drawn data. number variables consider split controlled n_vars parameter. lambda = 0, model trained using Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA). lambda > 0, model trained using Penalized Discriminant Analysis (PDA).

title: Trains a Random Forest of Projection-Pursuit oblique decision trees. — pprf
code: pprf(   formula = NULL,   data = NULL,   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   mode = NULL,   size = 100,   lambda = 0.5,   n_vars = NULL,   p_vars = NULL,   seed = NULL,   max_retries = 3L,   threads = NULL,   pp = NULL,   vars = NULL,   cutpoint = NULL,   stop = NULL,   binarize = NULL,   grouping = NULL,   leaf = NULL )

title: Trains a Random Forest of Projection-Pursuit oblique decision trees. — pprf
text: formula formula form y ~ x1 + x2 + ..., y vector labels x1, x2, ... features. data data frame containing variables formula. x matrix containing features observation. y matrix containing labels observation. mode Training mode: either "classification" "regression". NULL (default), mode auto-detected y's type — factor character vectors trigger classification, numeric vectors trigger regression. Setting explicitly useful binary-integer-labels case (mode = "classification" integer 0/1 labels) failing fast type mismatch (mode = "regression" factor y errors immediately). size number trees forest (default: 100). lambda regularization parameter (default: 0.5). lambda = 0, model trained using Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA). lambda > 0, model trained using Penalized Discriminant Analysis (PDA). default uses PDA pure LDA (lambda = 0) ill-conditioned variables effective observations (see "Known limitations" section README). used together pp. n_vars number variables consider split (integer). chosen uniformly split. default, half variables used (p_vars = 0.5). used together p_vars dr. p_vars proportion variables consider split (number 0 1, exclusive). example, p_vars = 0.5 uses half features. used together n_vars dr. seed optional integer seed reproducibility. NULL (default), seed drawn R's RNG, set.seed() controls reproducibility. integer provided, value used directly. seed used training computing permuted variable importance. max_retries Maximum number retries degenerate trees (default: 3). bootstrap sample yields singular covariance matrix, tree retrained different seed many times. threads number threads use. default number cores available. pp projection pursuit strategy object created pp_pda. used together lambda. vars variable selection strategy object created vars_uniform vars_all. used together n_vars p_vars. cutpoint split cutpoint strategy object created cutpoint_mean_of_means (default). stop stopping rule object. Default depends mode: stop_pure_node() classification, stop_any(stop_min_size(5), stop_min_variance(0.01)) regression. binarize binarization strategy object. Default depends mode: binarize_largest_gap() classification, binarize_disabled() regression (regression's default grouping always yields 2-group partition, binarization needed). grouping grouping strategy object. Default depends mode: grouping_by_label() classification, grouping_by_cutpoint() regression. leaf leaf strategy object. Default depends mode: leaf_majority_vote() classification, leaf_mean_response() regression.

title: Trains a Random Forest of Projection-Pursuit oblique decision trees. — pprf
text: pprf model. S3 class vector   c("pprf_classification", "pprf", "ppmodel")   c("pprf_regression", "pprf", "ppmodel") depending mode.

title: Trains a Random Forest of Projection-Pursuit oblique decision trees. — pprf
text: Mode taken mode argument explicit, otherwise auto-detected `y` (factor/character → classification, numeric → regression). Pass mode = "classification" force classification integer labels (e.g. binary 0/1), mode = "regression" assert intent numeric responses. OOB error, OOB predictions, permuted variable importance, weighted variable importance computed lazily first access via accessor functions (`oob_error()`, `oob_predictions()`, `permuted_importance()`, `weighted_importance()`). Training fast OOB-based computations deferred.

title: ppforest2 0.1.0
text: Core: Projection-pursuit oblique decision trees random forests classification, using LDA/PDA optimization. Core: Random uniform variable selection per split forest diversity. Core: Three variable importance measures: permuted (VI1), projections (VI2), weighted projections (VI3). Core: --bag error confusion matrix forests, bootstrap sample indices persisted recomputation. oob_error NA_real_ (R) / “available” (CLI) observation --bag tree. Core: Degenerate split detection projection-pursuit find useful projection. Core: OpenMP multi-threaded forest training. Core: JSON serialization trained models. Optional metrics fields use uniform null--value representation downstream tooling can distinguish “computed empty” shapes without special-casing. Core: Cross-platform reproducibility — identical results seed Linux, macOS, Windows, enforced golden-file tests CI. R: pptr() pprf() formula matrix interfaces. Returned models carry S3 class vector identifying model type mode (e.g. c("pprf_classification", "pprf", "ppmodel")). R: predict() returns group labels (type = "class") vote proportions (type = "prob") classification. R: summary() displays training OOB confusion matrices. R: Lazy OOB accessors — oob_error(), oob_predictions(), oob_samples(), bag_samples(), permuted_importance(), weighted_importance() — compute training data stored model first access memoize environment cache, training fast repeated access free. oob_predictions() returns factor NA rows OOB tree. R: Permuted variable importance may negative; meaningful signal (“within noise”) rather sentinel, callers rely ranking rather clipping zero. Weighted projection importance non-negative construction. R: Degenerate split warnings projection-pursuit separate groups. R: save_json() load_json() model persistence. R: tidymodels/parsnip integration: pp_tree() pp_rand_forest() model specifications. R: ggplot2 visualizations — tree diagrams, variable importance plots, projection histograms, decision boundary plots. R: Bundled classification datasets: crab, crabs, fishcatch, glass, image, leukemia, lymphoma, NCI60, olive, parkinson, wine. (Use datasets::iris base R iris examples.) CLI: train fits tree forest CSV saves JSON. CLI: predict applies saved model new data. CLI: evaluate runs train/test evaluation smart convergence. CLI: summarize displays model configuration, data summary, metrics saved model JSON. --data recomputes metrics training data. CLI: benchmark runs multi-scenario performance benchmarks baseline comparison. CLI: serve exposes saved model HTTP — GET / returns model summary JSON (HTML dashboard browsers showing configuration, training metrics, variable importance), GET /health liveness probe, POST /predict accepts feature CSV returns predictions (JSON API clients, HTML predictions page browsers, Download CSV button confusion matrix request CSV includes response column). Results cached -memory shareable ?id=… URLs; dashboard binds 127.0.0.1:8080 default.

title: ppforest2 0.1.0
text: Regression support included untested production workloads. API surface defaults may change future releases. Core: Regression training via ByCutpoint grouping strategy quantile-slices continuous response, MeanResponse leaf, MinSize / MinVariance / CompositeStop (stop::) stop rules. Core: Regression metrics — MSE, MAE, R² — computed training --bag predictions. Forest OOB error reported MSE. R: Regression auto-detected y numeric (factor). predict() returns numeric vector (type = "response"). R: Regression strategy wrappers: grouping_by_cutpoint(), leaf_mean_response(), stop_min_size(), stop_min_variance(), stop_any(). R: summary() displays MSE / MAE / R² regression models. oob_predictions() returns numeric vector NA_real_ rows OOB tree. R: save_json() / load_json() preserve regression mode; parsnip pp_tree() / pp_rand_forest() accept mode = "regression". CLI: --mode classification|regression selects training mode; regression reads last CSV column continuous response. predict returns numeric predictions MSE/MAE/R²; evaluate reports MSE regression. R: Bundled regression dataset california_housing (20,433 × 9, predict median_house_value). smaller regression examples use datasets::mtcars base R.
